summary: Matched two Mushroom Kingdom grKinokoRoute stage callback functions, replacing placeholders for grKinokoRoute_802084B4 and grKinokoRoute_80208660 with typed C implementations and updating their header prototypes. The first callback manipulates a stage JObj, marks another ground object flag, spawns effect 0x442 at the JObj position, triggers camera/stage/material side effects, and preserves stack padding. The second checks a fighter/object position and velocity threshold, clamps Y to 5.5F, calls grKinokoRoute_802086EC with a scale derived from ftLib_80086B80, triggers Ground_801C53EC, pads stack, and returns false. PR text states both functions verified 100% in objdiff and ninja built cleanly.

key_files.why_it_matters: Replaced two placeholder comments with matching implementations and added required includes for efSync, grMaterial, and HSD_JObj APIs.
Converted two unknown prototypes to typed signatures: grKinokoRoute_802084B4(HSD_GObj*) and grKinokoRoute_80208660(s32, HSD_GObj*) returning bool.

what_changed: Added includes in grkinokoroute.c for "ef/efsync.h", "gr/grmaterial.h", and <baselib/jobj.h> to support the newly matched calls.
Implemented grKinokoRoute_802084B4 as a void callback taking HSD_GObj*. It accesses gobj->user_data through a local anonymous struct with 0xDD8 bytes of padding before an HSD_JObj* field.
grKinokoRoute_802084B4 calls HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll(gp->jobj, 0x10), finds another ground object with Ground_801C2BA4(3), and if its user_data exists sets b0 on an UnkFlagStruct at offset 0xC4.

grKinokoRoute_802084B4 computes a Vec3 from the JObj with lb_8000B1CC, spawns effect 0x442 with efSync_Spawn, calls Camera_80030E44(2, NULL), Ground_801C5414(0x136, 0xBA), grMaterial_801C8CDC(gobj), and uses PAD_STACK(20).
Implemented grKinokoRoute_80208660 as bool grKinokoRoute_80208660(s32 arg0, HSD_GObj* gobj). The first argument is unused in the diff excerpt.

grKinokoRoute_80208660 reads position and velocity with ftLib_80086644 and ftLib_80086684, checks pos.y < 5.5F and vel.y > 5.5F, derives scale from ftLib_80086B80(gobj) / 10.0F, clamps pos.y to 5.5F, calls grKinokoRoute_802086EC(&pos, scale), triggers Ground_801C53EC(0x77A10), then returns false after PAD_STACK(8).
Header prototypes were updated from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete typed declarations for the two matched functions.
smart_moves: Used a small anonymous padded struct for gobj->user_data to expose only the required HSD_JObj* at offset 0xDD8 instead of prematurely naming or fully defining a large stage-specific data structure.
Preserved required stack shape with PAD_STACK(20) and PAD_STACK(8), a common matching tactic when otherwise-correct C needs explicit stack padding.
Kept side-effect order explicit in grKinokoRoute_802084B4: set JObj flags, mark related ground object state, compute effect position, spawn effect, update camera/stage/material state.
Improved type information in the header while leaving still-unmatched neighboring functions as UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS, avoiding overreach.
Guarded the secondary ground object's user_data before writing to an offset field, matching the control flow shown in the diff.
decomp_lessons: For stage callbacks, user_data often needs offset-based access before the full struct is known; anonymous padded structs can be a pragmatic bridge to a match.
When matching stage event logic, effect spawning, camera triggers, ground callbacks, and material updates may all be part of one callback and their exact order can be important.
ftLib position/velocity helpers can be used in ground route callbacks to detect player movement conditions without needing to name the full fighter data path.
Literal float thresholds and derived scale expressions, here 5.5F and ftLib_80086B80(gobj) / 10.0F, should be preserved exactly for matching and behavioral clarity.
Header cleanup should accompany matched functions by replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with the smallest evidenced concrete signature.
Explicit PAD_STACK usage remains acceptable when needed to preserve objdiff equivalence in matched functions.
naming_conventions: Stage route functions keep the address-style project naming form grKinokoRoute_8020xxxx until more semantic names are available.
Concrete callback prototypes use project types such as HSD_GObj*, Vec3*, f32, s32, and bool rather than generic UNK_PARAMS once evidence supports them.
Local temporaries in the diff use concise names such as gobj2, gp, gp2, sp_vec, pos, and vel; no broader semantic rename was attempted without stronger evidence.
The existing helper name grKinokoRoute_802086EC(Vec3*, f32) was reused as-is rather than renamed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Anonymous local struct with char pad[0xDD8] to reach a nested HSD_JObj* in gobj->user_data.
Offset cast to set ((UnkFlagStruct*)((u8*)gp2 + 0xC4))->b0 = 1 on another ground object's user_data.
PAD_STACK(20) in grKinokoRoute_802084B4 and PAD_STACK(8) in grKinokoRoute_80208660 to satisfy stack layout.
Maintained float constants as 5.5F and 10.0F rather than alternate double or integer expressions.
Used NULL arguments explicitly in lb_8000B1CC(gp->jobj, NULL, &sp_vec) and Camera_80030E44(2, NULL), matching call signatures and likely codegen.
Returned false from the bool callback rather than 0, while preserving the boolean return type.

review_feedback.text: The provided PR slice contains no review comments and no review records. Only the PR body/comment excerpt is available.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully name or define the grKinokoRoute user_data struct containing the HSD_JObj* at offset 0xDD8.
Search for other stage route callbacks that call efSync_Spawn with effect 0x442 or use Camera_80030E44 after JObj position lookup.
Search for other uses of Ground_801C2BA4(3) and UnkFlagStruct at user_data offset 0xC4 to infer the ground object state field name.
Compare grKinokoRoute_80208660 with other ftLib_80086644/ftLib_80086684 threshold callbacks to identify a reusable movement-trigger pattern.
Check whether PAD_STACK(20) or PAD_STACK(8) can be removed after future struct/type improvements without breaking matching.
